markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Test that legacy names route to UserManual or are explicitly deprecated without silent split-brain docs. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: LegacyManualShimTests leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LegacyManualShimTests is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Legacy manual bridge maps deprecated ModelManual route to canonical UserManual payload and emits compatibility receipt; proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: Legacy manual route returns deprecated/canonical bridge payload and records compatibility receipt while alias resolution keeps old names mapped to canonical UserManual pages.
MT-203 targeted API test passed against real PostgreSQL: legacy bridge returns deprecated/canonical payload and persists compatibility receipt; alias negative checks are covered by the suite.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Heisenberg read-only adversarial review returned PASS for MT-203 with no MT-blocking findings.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-203 requires legacy ModelManual names to route to canonical UserManual or be explicitly deprecated without silent split-brain docs, delivering the spec 10.15.8 legacy shim bridge: a legacy path may exist only if it maps deterministically to a UserManualRecord authority entry and emits a compatibility receipt when used. The code implements exactly this with a real legacy bridge route, a PostgreSQL-seeded deterministic alias mapping, a filesystem split-brain gate, and a real-PostgreSQL test that verifies the compatibility receipt is persisted in kernel_event_ledger.
findings: Spec 10.15.8 (current bundle, 10-product-surfaces.md:8151, [ADD v02.192], unchanged in v02.194): a legacy ModelManual path may remain ONLY if it (a) maps deterministically to a UserManualRecord authority entry and (b) emits a compatibility receipt when used. The implementation satisfies both halves.

Legacy bridge route src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/user_manual.rs:64-65,514-557: GET /usermanual/legacy/model-manual returns deprecated:true, a deprecation_note pointing to canonical /usermanual routes, the canonical mapping (route_namespace=/usermanual, pages, aliases), and a compatibility_receipt_event_id. GET /usermanual/legacy/aliases returns the mapping set.

Compatibility receipt is a REAL EventLedger write: api emits store.append_manual_receipt("legacy_bridge_used","model_manual",...) (user_manual.rs:522-534), which at store.rs:288-297 calls self.db.append_kernel_event(event) into the real kernel_event_ledger (event_type KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED). Not a mock.

Deterministic mapping is PostgreSQL-backed: 8 typed LEGACY_ALIASES (migration_plan.rs, alias count 8 >= test floor of 6) seeded via upsert_legacy_alias (seed.rs:1837) into table user_manual_legacy_aliases created by migration 0310_user_manual.sql:176. list_legacy_aliases (store.rs:927) reads them back.

Split-brain prevention gate is real: tests/user_manual_storage_tests.rs:54 mt193_every_legacy_model_manual_file_is_plan_covered scans the filesystem for any model_manual source/test file and fails if it lacks a typed PlanRow, enforcing the MT's 'no silent split-brain docs' requirement.

Real runtime proof: tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs:466 mt203_legacy_bridge_route_maps_and_emits_compat_receipt drives a live axum server over real PostgreSQL, asserts deprecated/canonical mapping, then at line 480-484 calls receipt_exists() which runs a direct SQL EXISTS query against kernel_event_ledger for the returned event_id. skip_if_no_pg! (user_manual_support.rs:133-143) PANICS when PG is absent rather than silently skipping, so PG proof is mandatory.

Green evidence log ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/green-mt203-legacy-bridge-proof.log exists and shows 'mt203_legacy_bridge_route_maps_and_emits_compat_receipt ... ok' in 4.49s against PostgreSQL at 127.0.0.1:5544 — non-trivial runtime, not a no-op.

Sub-rule-1: grep of api/user_manual.rs, migration_plan.rs, store.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable/placeholder/FIXME/stub returned NO hits on the legacy-bridge path.

Scope/boundary: commit fcf9bce3 touches only src/backend/handshake_core/** and tests/** (all within packet allowed_paths). No SQLite, no Docker, no external search engine, no markdown-vault-as-authority — authority is PostgreSQL/EventLedger.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): commit subject is prefixed 'gov(WP-KERNEL-009)' and packet/MT cite spec bundle v02.192/v02.193 while current is v02.194; per validation ground rules these paperwork/pointer notes are not failing reasons and the spec section resolves identically in the current bundle.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
